title: Find-or-merge block-api primitives lack typed-file generalization
description: FGAP-019 closure (commit 2160c63) generalized writeBlock and appendToBlock to typed-file primitives (writeTypedFile, appendToTypedFile) operating on arbitrary (filePath, schemaPath) pairs. The other six block-api primitives (updateItemInBlock, upsertItemInBlock, appendToNestedArray, updateNestedArrayItem, removeFromBlock, removeFromNestedArray) still hardcode .project/-block path resolution via blockFilePath/blockSchemaPath. Their refactor as wrappers (Phase C of Step 6.1) routes through writeBlock for the final write — which now goes through writeTypedFile — but the find-or-merge LOGIC stays hardcoded to .project/-targeting at the wrapper boundary. Asymmetric surface: typed-file primitives exist for whole-file write + array-append but not for find-or-merge / upsert / nested-append / nested-update / remove operations on non-.project/ paths.

evidence.reference: writeTypedFile + appendToTypedFile exported (lines 377, 442). updateItemInBlock, upsertItemInBlock, appendToNestedArray, updateNestedArrayItem, removeFromBlock, removeFromNestedArray exported but only as .project/-block-name primitives.

learnPattern uses dedup-then-append today (loadPatterns + check + appendToTypedFile). If the contract evolved to upsert-by-id (id-keyed pattern that should refresh learned_at on re-detection), no typed-file upsert primitive exists — would force re-implementing the find-or-merge logic outside block-api or revert to fs.writeFileSync.

impact: Future consumers of non-.project/ side-car state needing find-or-merge / upsert / nested-mutation semantics cannot reuse block-api's atomic critical-section + AJV + ctx-stamping infrastructure. Each such consumer either (a) re-implements the read-mutate-write loop with its own lock/validate/stamp logic — recreating FGAP-019, or (b) constructs a synthetic block name pointing outside .project/ — abuse of the wrapper boundary. The architectural symmetry FGAP-019 closure was meant to deliver (one canonical surface for ALL framework writes) is delivered for whole-file + simple-append only.
proposed_resolution: Extract typed-file equivalents for the remaining six find-or-merge primitives: updateItemInTypedFile, upsertItemInTypedFile, appendToNestedTypedFile, updateNestedItemInTypedFile, removeFromTypedFile, removeFromNestedTypedFile. Each follows the same generalization pattern as Phase A of Step 6.1 — operate on (filePath, schemaPath, ...) pairs, withBlockLock wraps the read-mutate-write critical section, ctx-stamping via maybeStampItem (already schemaPath-keyed per Phase B). Existing .project/-targeting primitives become wrappers as in Phase C. ~+200 net lines; test surface ~+10 typed-file find-or-merge tests + wrapper-continuity smoke. No live regression — purely architectural-symmetry completion of Step 6.1.
